title: QPU Architect
description: eleQtron develops and operates full-stack quantum computers based on trapped-ion technology. Quantum computing is a new computing paradigm rapidly approaching commercial viability. Our unique approach combines laser-cooled trapped ions with microwave control and tailored magnetic fields. Our next-generation computing machines under development will be able to find solutions to problems that are out of reach today, thus enabling new innovative products and services for a brighter future. Responsibilities include compiler architecture, software optimisation, HW/SW co-design, performance analysis of quantum algorithms, benchmark definition, roadmap support, and compiler design. Required skills include Python/Rust/C++, Qiskit or Cirq, quantum algorithms, quantum circuit design, and quantum error correction knowledge.

title: Quantum Engineer
description: Quantum Engineer role at a deep-tech startup building the world's most sensitive magnetic microscope. Responsibilities include optical system development, building laser-based optical setups, optical simulations (Zemax), quantum measurements on diamond sensors, experimental protocol development, large dataset analysis with Python or MATLAB, system integration, and troubleshooting. Requires strong experimental physics background, optics expertise, and data analysis skills.

title: Quantum Error Correction Engineer
description: Quantum Error Correction Engineer at eleQtron working on trapped-ion quantum computers. Responsibilities include development and implementation of quantum error correction codes, optimization of gate sequences and decoding strategies, noise characterization, benchmarking, implementation of QEC protocols, and translating research into practical optimizations. Requires PhD or MSc with strong experience, knowledge of surface codes, stabilizer formalism, Python, and frameworks like Qiskit, Cirq, or QuTiP.
